# QSO Predictor

> A free, open-source desktop tactical assistant for FT8 and FT4 amateur
> radio operators. Reconstructs the DX station's perspective using PSK
> Reporter spot data so you can see the pileup *they're* hearing, find
> frequencies clear at *their* location, and confirm propagation paths
> before calling. Runs on Windows, macOS, and Linux alongside WSJT-X or
> JTDX via UDP integration. Advisory only — the operator keeps full
> control of the radio.

QSO Predictor is written by Peter Hirst (WU2C), licensed under GPLv3.
Built in Python with PyQt6. Distribution: free GitHub releases (Windows
zip, signed-and-notarized macOS dmg, source for all platforms) and a
Microsoft Store listing (Windows only, easiest install).

The app does not collect, transmit, or store any personal data. It reads
local WSJT-X/JTDX log files, subscribes read-only to the public PSK
Reporter MQTT stream, and fetches space weather from NOAA SWPC. No
telemetry. No tracking.

## Primary documentation

- [User Guide](https://qsop.wu2c.net/user-guide/): Installation, configuration, and how to use each feature of the app.
- [Home page](https://qsop.wu2c.net/): Project overview, installation options, key features.

## Technical and deeper documentation

- [How and Why It Works](https://github.com/wu2c-peter/QSO-Predictor/wiki/QSO-Predictor-How-and-Why-It-Works): Algorithms, architecture, and design rationale.
- [Quick Usage Guide](https://github.com/wu2c-peter/QSO-Predictor/wiki/Quick-Usage-Guide): Operational workflows for common scenarios.
- [Help and Troubleshooting](https://github.com/wu2c-peter/QSO-Predictor/wiki/Help-and-Troubleshooting): Common problems and solutions.
- [Source code on GitHub](https://github.com/wu2c-peter/qso-predictor): Repository, issue tracker, contributing guidelines.

## Releases and changes

- [Latest release](https://github.com/wu2c-peter/qso-predictor/releases/latest): Download Windows .zip and macOS .dmg.
- [Release history](https://github.com/wu2c-peter/QSO-Predictor/releases): All published versions with release notes.

## Optional

- [Privacy Policy](https://qsop.wu2c.net/privacy/): What data is read, why, and where it stays (locally, in all cases).
- [Support](https://qsop.wu2c.net/support/): Bug reports, feature requests, and direct contact.
